audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim false-sign, private enticement, and apostate-city law, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for sign-working prophet tests, intimate enticement, mandatory denunciation, public execution, apostate-city investigation, total destruction, and anti-persecution safeguards.

verses.restored: All word I command you, it you shall keep to do; you shall not add to it or diminish from it.
If a prophet or dreamer of dream arises in your midst and gives you sign or wonder,
and sign or wonder comes that he spoke to you, saying, Let us go after other gods you have not known and let us serve them,
you shall not listen to words of that prophet or dreamer of dream, for YHWH your Divine is testing you to know whether you love YHWH your Divine with all your heart and all your soul/life.
After YHWH your Divine you shall walk, him you shall fear/revere, his commands you shall keep, his voice you shall listen to, him you shall serve, and to him you shall cling.

That prophet or dreamer of dream shall be put to death, because he spoke rebellion against YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt and redeemed you from house of slaves, to thrust you from way YHWH your Divine commanded you to walk. So you shall remove evil from your midst.

If your brother, son of your mother, or your son or daughter, or wife of your embrace, or your friend who is as your own soul/life secretly entices you, saying, Let us go and serve other gods, which you have not known, you or your fathers,
from gods of peoples around you, near to you or far from you, from one end of earth to other end of earth,
you shall not consent to him or listen to him; your eye shall not pity him, and you shall not spare or conceal him.
But you shall surely kill him; your hand shall be first against him to put him to death, and afterward hand of all people.
You shall stone him with stones and he shall die, because he sought to thrust you away from YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt, from house of slaves.
All Israel shall hear and fear/revere and shall not again do according to this evil thing in your midst.
If you hear in one of your cities YHWH your Divine gives you to dwell there, saying,
Men, sons of worthlessness, have gone out from your midst and thrust away inhabitants of their city, saying, Let us go and serve other gods you have not known,
then you shall inquire and search and ask diligently; and look, if truth is established, the thing certain, this abomination was done in your midst,
you shall surely strike inhabitants of that city with edge of sword, devoting it and all in it to destruction, and its livestock with edge of sword.
All its spoil you shall gather into midst of its square and burn city and all its spoil entirely with fire to YHWH your Divine; it shall be a ruin forever and shall not be built again.
Nothing from devoted thing shall cling to your hand, so YHWH may turn from burning anger and give you compassion and show you compassion and multiply you, as he swore to your fathers,
when you listen to voice of YHWH your Divine, keeping all his commands I command you today, doing right in eyes of YHWH your Divine.

translation_notes: A sign or wonder is not treated as self-authenticating; allegiance and ethical covenant context matter more than spectacle.
The chapter escalates from false prophet, to intimate family enticement, to an entire accused city.
Its death penalties and city-annihilation command are religious-persecution texts of extreme moral danger and must never be reenacted.
The city case at least requires diligent investigation and established truth, but that due-process element does not make collective destruction morally acceptable.
'Sons of worthlessness' is a polemical label, not permission to dehumanize dissenters or minority religions.
The Exodus liberation frame is used here to enforce exclusive worship; modern reception must not convert liberation memory into coercive religious violence.
safety_note: Never use Deuteronomy 13 to justify execution for belief, apostasy laws, family denunciation, religious policing, mob violence, destruction of towns, or persecution of converts, atheists, queer people, or minority faiths. Preserve its warning against manipulation by spectacle while categorically rejecting coercive enforcement and collective punishment.
